item.title: Create docs/CHANGELOG.md — retroactive changelog from build history
item.intent: A project with no changelog looks like it was built in one shot — the changelog proves sustained development and helps evaluators gauge velocity and direction.
item.description: No CHANGELOG.md exists and no version tags are in the repo. However, build history can be reconstructed from: backlog/GAP-ANALYSIS.md 'AS-IS: What's Built' (subsystem status table with line counts showing what has shipped), docs/PRODUCT.md checkbox items showing completed features, backlog/archive/sprints.jsonl (sprint completion records with dates and item IDs), and backlog/backlog.json + bugs.json (done items with completedAt timestamps). Create a retroactive changelog organized by development phase rather than version numbers (since no versions exist yet). Phase 1: Foundation (config schema, landmine validator, gateway client, workspace generator). Phase 2: Blueprints (blueprint engine, 7 blueprints, skill system, 6 skills). Phase 3: Platform (install pipeline, security hardening, doctor/status, cron engine). Include dates where available from completedAt timestamps.
steps: Read backlog/GAP-ANALYSIS.md 'AS-IS: What's Built' section for the subsystem status table
Read backlog/backlog.json done items with completedAt timestamps to establish timeline
Read backlog/archive/sprints.jsonl for sprint completion records with dates
Read docs/PRODUCT.md 'What We're Building' checkbox items for completed feature list
Compose docs/CHANGELOG.md organized by development phase: Phase 1 Foundation (with dates from completedAt), Phase 2 Blueprints, Phase 3 Platform, using Added/Changed/Fixed format within each phase
Include line counts from GAP-ANALYSIS where available to show scope of each phase
acceptanceCriteria: docs/CHANGELOG.md exists with retroactive entries organized by development phase
Each phase lists completed features with dates where available
Entries use Added/Changed/Fixed format consistent with Keep a Changelog convention
The changelog accurately reflects what has been built — no aspirational entries
